title: Sindromul Gigel Paraschiv sau de ce se găsește greu un ministru al Educației
summary: Political scientist Cristian Preda critiques the chronic dysfunction in Romania's education system, centered on the 14-year tenure of state secretary Gigel Paraschiv, who symbolizes institutional tolerance of rector power abuses and plagiarism. Preda argues that PM Bolojan's government has started poorly on education reform, appointing compromised figures to key advisory positions.
talking_points: Gigel Paraschiv has served as state secretary for education for nearly 14 years continuously, enabling rector abuses and plagiarism
University rectors illegally extend their mandates beyond the legal two-term limit, with some reaching four or five terms
The current Bolojan government has appointed compromised figures like Marilen Pirtea, Ligia Deca, and Sorin Costreie to education-related positions
Romania's university budget allocation is incomparably smaller than other EU member states, intensifying internal power struggles over scarce resources
True education reform will only begin when Paraschiv is replaced and the cycle of power abuse and plagiarism defense ends
concepts: abuse of power in academic institutions
author: Cristian Preda
